summarization.a: The figure shows OES spectra of an O2 plasma in saturation and shortly after TMA dosing in an ALD cycle.
summarization.b: The figure displays the intensity of O, H, O2+, CO, and OH over time, where the plasma is struck after a TMA dose.

data_extraction.a:
| Wavelength (nm) | Intensity (a.u.) O2 plasma |Intensity (a.u.) O2 plasma after ALD cycle |
|---|---|---|
| 260-350 | - | OH range |
| 350-720 | - | CO range |
| 560 | O2+ peak |O2+ peak |
| 610 | - | H peak |
| 780 | O peak |O peak |

a.question: How can you tell that there are mostly molecular species coming from the ligands?
a.answer: a	The spectrum related to the plasma after the TMA dose shows a broad range of emissions. Such a range usually denoted emission from molecular species.

a.question: At what wavelength can a hydrogen line be found?
a.answer: a	A hydrogen line can be found at 660 nm.

b.question: Based on this graph, what is the saturated oxygen plasma exposure time and how can you tell?
b.answer: b	The saturated plasma exposure time is 2 s. At this time there is no more intensity measured of ligand related species.

b.question: What atoms are likely in the ligands based on the graph?
b.answer: b	The ligands are composed of C and H atoms.
